criterion_scores.critique:
Sub-attribute entity_coverage:
  - Tenant entity present? YES. Evidence: "tenants" array with tenant objects containing id, name, status
  - Plan entity present? YES. Evidence: "plans" array with plan objects containing id, name, pricing_model
  - Subscription entity present? YES. Evidence: "subscriptions" array with subscription objects containing id, tenant_id, plan_id, status
  - Usage record entity present? YES. Evidence: "usage_records" array with usage record objects containing id, subscription_id, metered_dimension_id, quantity
  - Invoice entity present? YES. Evidence: "invoices" array with invoice objects containing id, tenant_id, subscription_id, line_items
  - Line item entity present? YES. Evidence: "line_item" definition with id, description, type, quantity, unit_amount, total_amount
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: includes subscription_changes entity for tracking plan changes, upgrades, downgrades which shows deeper billing domain understanding

Sub-attribute pricing_model_support:
  - Seat-based pricing model present? YES. Evidence: pricing_model.type enum includes "seat_based" with seat_price field
  - Usage-based pricing model present? YES. Evidence: pricing_model.type enum includes "usage_based" with metered_dimensions array
  - Hybrid pricing model present? YES. Evidence: pricing_model.type enum includes "hybrid" with conditional logic requiring both seat_price and metered_dimensions
  - Tiered pricing support present? YES. Evidence: pricing_tier definition with start_at, end_at, unit_price for usage tiers
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: sophisticated conditional validation using if/then/else for different pricing model requirements, showing advanced JSON Schema knowledge

Sub-attribute billing_lifecycle:
  - Billing cycles present? YES. Evidence: billing_interval enum with "monthly", "quarterly", "annually"
  - Proration support present? YES. Evidence: proration_behavior enum with "none", "create_prorations", "always_invoice"
  - Trial periods present? YES. Evidence: trial_start, trial_end fields in subscriptions and trial_days in pricing_model
  - Upgrades/downgrades present? YES. Evidence: subscription_changes entity with change_type enum including "upgrade", "downgrade", "seat_change", "plan_change"
  - Subscription status lifecycle present? YES. Evidence: subscription_status enum with "trialing", "active", "past_due", "cancelled", "unpaid", "paused"
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: includes sophisticated proration_invoice_id tracking and effective_date handling for subscription changes

Sub-attribute schema_validity:
  - Valid JSON Schema draft-07 syntax? YES. Evidence: "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#"
  - Proper type definitions throughout? YES. Evidence: consistent use of "type": "object", "type": "string", "type": "array" etc.
  - Valid enum definitions? YES. Evidence: multiple enums like billing_interval, entity_status, subscription_status
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? NO. Evidence: follows standard JSON Schema patterns without exceptional innovation

Sub-attribute ref_usage:
  - Uses $ref for reusable definitions? YES. Evidence: "$ref": "#/definitions/auditable_entity", "$ref": "#/definitions/money", "$ref": "#/definitions/uuid"
  - Avoids duplication through references? YES. Evidence: auditable_entity referenced by multiple entities, money type reused for all monetary fields
  - Proper definitions section structure? YES. Evidence: comprehensive definitions section with reusable types like timestamp, uuid, money
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: sophisticated use of allOf to compose inheritance-like patterns with auditable_entity

Sub-attribute constraint_quality:
  - Proper enum constraints present? YES. Evidence: multiple enums like billing_interval, entity_status, subscription_status, line_item_type
  - Required fields specified? YES. Evidence: required arrays on all major entities like ["id", "name", "status"] for tenants
  - Format validation present? YES. Evidence: "format": "date-time" for timestamps, "format": "uuid" for idempotency keys
  - Pattern validation present? YES. Evidence: regex patterns like "^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}$" for UUIDs
  - Numerical constraints present? YES. Evidence: "minimum": 0 on monetary amounts, "minItems": 1 on arrays
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: sophisticated pattern validation like "^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$" for field names and complex conditional validation rules

Sub-attribute metered_extensibility:
  - Usage dimensions are dynamic array? YES. Evidence: metered_dimensions as array of metered_dimension objects with configurable id, name, unit, aggregation_type
  - Not hardcoded dimension fields? YES. Evidence: no hardcoded fields like "api_calls" or "storage_gb", uses generic dimension structure
  - Supports adding new dimensions without schema change? YES. Evidence: array structure allows adding new dimension objects
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: includes sophisticated pricing_tier structure within dimensions for complex tiered pricing

Sub-attribute metadata_support:
  - Custom metadata object on key entities? YES. Evidence: "metadata": {"$ref": "#/definitions/advanced_metadata"} on tenants, plans, subscriptions, invoices
  - Advanced metadata structure present? YES. Evidence: advanced_metadata definition with custom_fields, integration_data, workflow_state, audit_metadata
  - Extensible custom fields support? YES. Evidence: custom_fields with patternProperties allowing arbitrary field names with type validation
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: sophisticated metadata structure with integration_data patterns and workflow_state for business process support

Sub-attribute versioning:
  - Schema version field present? YES. Evidence: "version": "1.0.0" at root level and schema_version in auditable_entity
  - Versioning strategy documented? YES. Evidence: backward_compatibility section with supported_versions and api_version_requirements
  - Migration support indicated? YES. Evidence: migration_notes with breaking_changes and deprecation_timeline fields
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: comprehensive versioning strategy with entity-level schema versions and API version requirements mapping

- Violation "no_currency_handling": ABSENT. Evidence: currency fields present with ISO 4217 pattern "^[A-Z]{3}$" in money definition and invoices
  - Violation "calculated_total_without_line_items": ABSENT. Evidence: invoices have both line_items array (minItems: 1) and separate total/subtotal fields
  - Violation "no_idempotency": ABSENT. Evidence: idempotency_key fields present on subscriptions, usage_records, invoices, subscription_changes
  - Violation "no_invoice_status_lifecycle": ABSENT. Evidence: invoice_status enum with "draft", "open", "paid", "void", "uncollectible"
  - Violation "naive_date_handling": ABSENT. Evidence: proper timestamp definition using "format": "date-time" throughout
  - Violation "single_currency_assumption": ABSENT. Evidence: currency field on invoices and money definition supports multiple currencies
